title: Middlemarch
author: Eliot, George
summary: Dorothea Brooke, a young, idealistic woman at Tipton Grange, seeks a noble purpose beyond provincial life. Educated and passionate, she fears frivolity and yearns for a spiritual or intellectual mission, like Saint Theresa’s historic fervor. Her sensible sister Celia and their indulgent uncle, Mr. Brooke, contrast with Dorothea’s seriousness. Local society regards her eccentric; Sir James Chettam and others visit. The arrival of the scholarly, wealthy Reverend Edward Casaubon excites expectation. George Eliot frames Dorothea’s ardor as emblematic of earnest women whose grand impulses often clash with limited social opportunities and domestic realities in small English towns, and moral perplexities.
